violations.text: A "hot-shot" insecticide device was observed hanging on the shelving in the kitchen area. Person in charge removed device from the area at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The single door lowboy was observed in poor repair (door not sealing properly/gasket bent). New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The following must be cleaned and sanitized: 1) The floors in the back of the kitchen were observed with old rodent droppings 2) The floors under the 3-bay were observed with debris and dog treats New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
No person in charge certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the cafe was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, chlorine sanitizing solution was observed to be empty. Person in charge connected new sanitizing solution to read between 50-100ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine in the kitchen and cafe have an accumulation of biofilm. The non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent possible contamination of ice and biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

Dishes and utensils were observed in the sink labeled as a hand sink at the coffee car counter. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC to maintain one of the sinks at the Coffee Bar counter as a dedicated hand sink Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the Coffee Bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Utensils/Equipment to be properly sanitized at the Lounge Bar dish machine or the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

At the time of inspection, Chemical spray bottles were observed stored hanging from the faucet controls of the Food Prep Sink. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Chemical Spray Bottles were moved to the floor area under the sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

At the time of inspection, the dish machine at the Coffee Bar Counter was observed not dispensing sanitizer solution. Unit is to be adjusted/fixed prior to reuse for proper sanitizing of equipment/utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023
